summary.text: [0-8] A static shot shows a silver metallic bar on patterned fabric, featuring a loose black rocker switch on the left and a circular socket cutout on the right. Centered is a plastic bag containing a switch labeled "#R561117 ROCKER SWITCH BLACK ALLURE 1" with text "MADE IN CHINA PACKAGED IN MEXICO". [8-10] The camera tilts up rapidly, revealing a stainless steel stove control panel with silver knobs and a digital display, while the parts below blur. [10-14] The view settles on a mounted stainless steel range hood under white cabinets. The hood has a black control panel with buttons, and its exposed undercarriage shows blue, yellow, and black wires hanging against a white tiled wall.

[14-24] Zooming in on a stainless steel range hood's black control panel labeled "ALLURE" and "by NuTone", the view shows a "fan" rocker switch (low/high) and an empty "light" cutout. Tilting down reveals exposed blue, yellow, and black wires with red connectors, then shows a stainless steel stove with silver knobs and a digital clock displaying "1:13".
[24-28] The view settles on a silver panel where a plastic bag sits next to a circular socket; the bag is labeled "#R561117 ROCKER SWITCH BLACK ALLURE 1" with "MADE IN CHINA" and "PACKAGED IN MEXICO" visible.

Throughout the duration from t=28s to t=40s, the camera maintains a fixed, high-angle close-up on a clear plastic bag resting on a silver metallic work surface. The bag features a prominent white label with the identifier "#R561117" and the description "ROCKER SWITCH BLACK ALLURE 1". Beneath a vertical barcode, the numbers "28715 14676" are printed, along with "61B17T" and "990724775B" in the corner. The lower section of the label states "MADE IN CHINA" and "PACKAGED IN MEXICO". To the right of the bag, a circular cutout in the metal panel is visible. The scene is static with no camera movement, lighting shifts, or user interaction observed.

[42-46] The camera pulls back to reveal a silver metallic panel. On the left lies a loose black rocker switch with three vertical metal prongs. In the center rests a clear plastic bag labeled "#R561117 ROCKER SWITCH BLACK ALLURE 1", "MADE IN CHINA", "PACKAGED IN MEXICO", and barcode "26115 14676". To the right is a circular socket cutout.
[46-50] The view widens, showing the panel on patterned fabric. The camera tilts up, revealing a black trim piece and the upper appliance section. The loose switch, labeled bag, and socket cutout remain stationary.
[50-54] The camera pulls back further to show a white stove control panel with knobs and a digital display featuring text like "Warm Zone", "On", and "Off" above the silver strip. A white pitcher briefly appears on the right before the camera pans left, centering the view on the stove controls. The loose black switch, labeled bag, and circular cutout remain on the lower metallic surface.

[54-56] The camera pans left, bringing a black and yellow tool handle into the left foreground. The white appliance panel and silver strip shift rightward. The loose black switch and labeled plastic bag remain on the metallic surface. [56-66] The view zooms in tightly on the loose black rocker switch lying on the reflective silver panel, showing three metal prongs. The clear plastic bag is cropped on the right edge. A strong vertical glare reflects on the metal surface beside the switch. The camera holds a steady close-up, keeping the three brass-colored prongs distinct against the shiny surface. The edge of the clear plastic bag remains visible on the right, and lighting reflections persist without significant change.

From 66-74s, the camera maintains a close-up on a black rocker switch with three brass-colored prongs resting on a shiny silver metal surface. Bright vertical light reflections persist on the panel next to the switch and the edge of a clear plastic bag. At 74s, the perspective shifts and the switch is removed from the surface. The plastic bag, labeled #R561117, is visible near a circular socket cutout on the metallic strip. By 76s, the camera pulls back further to reveal a yellow flashlight lying on the patterned fabric below and a stainless steel stove control panel with silver knobs and a digital display in the background.

[78-82] The camera pans right across a patterned cloth to a grey countertop, revealing a blue-handled screwdriver, a black power drill, and a white bowl with black rim stripes containing four small silver screws. A white electric kettle is visible on the far right. [82-84] Tilting up shows a white toaster next to the kettle. [84-90] The view shifts to the underside of a stainless steel range hood mounted against a white tiled wall. Exposed blue, yellow, and black wires with red connectors hang centrally. To the left, a black fan assembly sits inside the housing near a white label with text and a barcode. The camera pans slightly right, revealing a white cabinet side panel.

[90-94] The camera pans slightly left and tilts up, bringing the black fan assembly within the stainless steel range hood housing into view. A label with "HVI" is visible on the unit. Blue, yellow, and black wires with red connectors hang below against a white tiled wall, shifting slightly right. The upper edge of the hood and a dark control panel area enter the top of the frame.
[94-102] The camera holds a steady shot then pans slowly right. The black fan motor assembly remains visible on the left side within the metal housing. The hanging wires stay central against the white tiled backsplash. A white cabinet side panel appears and becomes more prominent on the right side of the frame.

[102-106] Camera pans right from the stainless steel range hood underside, revealing a black fan assembly and hanging blue, yellow, and black wires with red connectors. A white cabinet door with a framed picture and hanging items comes into view. [106-110] Continuing right and tilting up, the view shows white cabinets (glass-front and solid) in an adjacent room, topped with an orange pot, silver cookware, and a black container. [110-114] The camera tilts up and zooms in on the range hood's interior, focusing on a black terminal block with red/black wires and red/blue crimp connectors near an oval light fixture. A label reading "AVI" is visible on the hood's side.

[114-120] The camera focuses on the range hood's internal wiring, where red and black wires connect to a black terminal block via red and blue crimp connectors above a small oval light fixture. The view pulls back and tilts down, blurring the stainless steel edge as white kitchen cabinets become visible below. [120-124] The camera tilts up to the front control panel, a black strip on the stainless steel housing featuring "ALLURE" text on the left and "by NuTone" on the right.
